summary.intent: Make the gossamer indexer/poll loop more robust by moving worktree and DB paths into the git root, then extend indexing to cover entire.io shadow branches and update the sessions UI to show both live and committed sessions.
summary.outcome: Worktree now lives at `.gossamer/checkpoints` and DB at `.gossamer/hook-handler.db` inside the repo root; shadow branch indexing polls every 5s using `git cat-file blob` (no worktree); sessions table shows 16 sessions (4 live, 12 checkpoint) with intent column and live badge.

repo: getDb() in src/db.ts already calls mkdirSync(dirname(abs), { recursive: true }), so callers don't need to pre-create directories.
Shadow branches follow the pattern `entire/<commit-short-hash>-<something>` and contain both `.gossamer/checkpoints/` and `.entire/metadata/<session-id>/` paths.
The same session transcript appears across multiple shadow branches as commits accumulate, so deduplication must track all seen blob SHAs (not just the latest) per session.
FTS tables in the SQLite DB are virtual tables managed by application code, not Prisma schema — prisma db push treats them as data loss but they rebuild automatically on startup.
The legacy Session table is no longer the source of truth; sessions are now reconstructed from CheckpointSessionMetadata + ShadowSession joined on sessionId.

code.finding: mkdirSync(dirname(abs), { recursive: true }) is called here, making directory pre-creation in callers redundant.
indexFullJsonl was refactored into two functions: indexFullJsonl (file path) and indexFullJsonlContent (string content), both accepting optional shadowSessionId.
ShadowSession.seenBlobShas is stored as a JSON array string to track all blob SHAs seen across branches for a session, preventing re-indexing.
WS change detector was updated to watch logEvent max id and shadowSession max id instead of the legacy event table.

workflow: When adding git worktrees, old registrations at previous paths must be explicitly pruned via `git worktree remove` or `git worktree prune` before new paths work.
Using `git cat-file blob <ref>:<path>` lets you read file contents from branches without checking them out into a worktree.
Deleting the SQLite DB and re-running prisma db push is the cleanest way to handle schema resets when all data rebuilds from git on startup.
Starting the server in background and polling logs with sleep lets you verify startup indexing behavior without blocking the terminal.

friction: Old worktree registration at /private/tmp/gossamer-checkpoints blocked new worktree creation and required manual pruning before the server could start cleanly.
Prisma db push prompted about FTS virtual tables as 'data loss' requiring --accept-data-loss flag, which is misleading since those tables rebuild automatically.
The server crashed on first start because the DB was partially created before the schema was pushed — required deleting the partial DB and repushing.
Blob SHA deduplication bug caused the same session to be re-indexed on every poll (different SHA per branch) until seenBlobShas array approach was implemented.
mapSession helper was left as dead code after the GET /api/sessions rewrite and required a second build-catch-fix cycle to remove.
open_items: The legacy Session table still exists in the schema but is no longer consulted — it should eventually be dropped to avoid confusion.
Shadow branch indexing uses local `git branch` which only sees branches that have been fetched; remote-only shadow branches won't be indexed without a fetch step.
No mechanism to clean up ShadowSession records when a shadow branch is merged into entire/checkpoints/v1 and the branch is deleted.
The 5s shadow polling interval is hardcoded — could be made configurable alongside the checkpoint polling interval.
